title: Figma Projects Config
description: Workspace-level configuration that maps repositories (or packages) to Figma files and drives the SpecKit Figma extension. Supports single-repo (default), mono-repo (Nx / Turborepo / pnpm / yarn / Lerna) and multi-repo (git submodules) layouts.

mode.description: Repository topology. 'single-repo' (default) and 'mono-repo' both use the single 'repo' object; 'multi-repo' uses the 'submodules' map. A 'single-repo' holds one front-end target; a 'mono-repo' hosts several internal packages (apps/libs).

apiBaseUrl.description: Base URL of the Figma REST API. Restricted to https://*.figma.com: this file is committed/shared, and any other host would receive the access token. Use the FIGMA_API_BASE environment variable for a local (trusted) override, e.g. an enterprise proxy.

contextSource.description: Design-context engine. 'rest' (default) is fully portable and CI-friendly (curl + jq against the Figma REST API). 'mcp' uses a Model Context Protocol Figma server for richer context when available, and automatically falls back to 'rest' when the MCP server is unreachable (unless mcp.fallbackToRest is false).

verifyStrict.description: When true, figma-verify-section.sh (run after generation, e.g. in CI) exits non-zero if a detected Figma mockup's design section is missing from the generated spec/plan/tasks document. Default false keeps the post-generation check non-blocking; the agent self-corrects instead.

mcp.description: Optional MCP server settings, used only when contextSource = 'mcp'. The extension probes the server and falls back to the REST engine when it is absent, preserving portability.

url.description: HTTP(S) endpoint of the Figma MCP server (e.g. the local Figma Dev Mode MCP server).

serverName.description: Logical name of the MCP server as registered with the agent (informational; helps the agent pick the right MCP tools).

fallbackToRest.description: When true (default), the extension transparently falls back to the REST engine if the MCP server is unreachable. When false, an unreachable MCP server is treated as a hard error.

source.description: 'env' reads the token from an environment variable (or .env locally). 'ci-secret' reads it from a CI/Cloud-Agent secret store. The token MUST NEVER be stored in this file.

envVar.description: Name of the environment variable holding the Figma Personal Access Token. Also used with source = 'ci-secret': it names the variable the CI injects the secret into (e.g. env: FIGMA_PAT: ${{ secrets.<secretName> }}).

secretName.description: Name of the CI/Cloud-Agent secret in the secret store when source = 'ci-secret' (e.g. a GitHub Actions secret). The token itself is read from the environment variable named by envVar; secretName is only used as the variable name when envVar is unset.

submodules.description: Multi-repo only. Keyed by git submodule / repository name.

repo.description: Single-repo and mono-repo only. Describes the single repository (and, for a mono-repo, its internal packages).

excluded.description: Repositories or packages for which Figma integration MUST be bypassed silently (back-end, infra, BFF, etc.).

enabled.description: Whether Figma integration is active for this target.

role.description: Architectural role. 'design-system' is the shared UI inventory; 'app-host' is a mono-repo containing multiple apps; 'app'/'lib' are leaf packages.

figmaFileId.description: Figma file key. MUST be a real key; placeholders starting with 'REPLACE_WITH_' are rejected by validation. Optional when a project or team id is provided for autonomous discovery.

figmaProjectId.description: Optional Figma project id. When set, the agent can autonomously enumerate all files/pages of the project.

figmaTeamId.description: Optional Figma team id. When set, the agent can autonomously enumerate every project of the team, then every file/page of each project — covering a full Figma organization > team > projects > files hierarchy.

figmaTeamIds.description: Optional list of Figma team ids when a single target spans several teams of the same organization. Each team is enumerated for its projects and files.

submodulePath.description: Filesystem path of the target within the workspace (submodule path or package path).

monorepo.description: Internal layout of the target when it hosts multiple packages.

designSystem.description: Cross-reference to the Design System used for component-reuse resolution and token mapping.

ref.description: Key of the Design System target (multi-repo) it refers to.

tokenSource.description: Path/glob to the DS token definitions (e.g. design tokens JSON, theme files).

pageToPackageMapping.description: Maps Figma page names to a target package. 'targetPackage: null' forces routing-rule resolution. 'shared: true' marks frames common to several apps.

sharedAcross.description: List of packages that share this page's frames (e.g. product detail page add-to-cart + user account header shared across several apps).

place.description: Resolution target. 'ask' pauses and asks the developer when the rule is ambiguous.
